Good work on the lessons learned document, Fabiana. The root cause analysis is solid.
Thanks. My biggest takeaway is that if we had set clearer benchmarks, we would not be in this situation today.
Agreed. The vendors inspection process was clearly flawed. If their protocol were stronger, the defective units would never have reached us.
The corrective action was effective, but I think the preventive measures are what will really make a difference. Quarterly audits with measurable benchmarks.
If we were better at early detection, we would have caught this during the rollout, not after. That is the key lesson.
Exactly. If we follow through on these recommendations, situations like this would not happen again. I will share the final document by Friday.
If we had implemented the system, we would not be dealing with errors today.
If we were more proactive about audits, we would not have ended up here.
The root cause was a flawed quality control process at the vendor.
We need quarterly vendor audits with measurable benchmarks.
If we follow through on these recommendations, this would not happen again.

Alright team, let us walk through the post-mortem of the APAC terminal rollout. If we had implemented the automated tracking system last year, we would not be dealing with these manual errors today. That is a mixed conditional: a past action affecting our present situation. Looking at the root cause analysis, the vendor's quality control process was flawed. If the vendor had run proper inspections, the defective units would not be sitting in our warehouse right now. On the positive side, if we had not caught the issue when we did, we would still be shipping faulty equipment to clients. The corrective action plan is clear: first, we need a revised inspection protocol. Second, if we were more proactive about vendor audits, we would not have ended up in this situation. The lessons learned document must capture all of this. I want every recommendation tied to a specific root cause.

Hi everyone, I have drafted the lessons learned document for the APAC rollout. Here are the key takeaways. First, if we had established clearer quality benchmarks from the start, the defective units would not have passed initial inspection. That is our biggest finding. Second, our corrective action was effective but late. If we were better at early detection, we would catch these issues before they reach the warehouse. Third, the root cause was not just the vendor. If our internal audit process had been stronger, we would be in a much better position today. My recommendation is to implement quarterly vendor audits with measurable benchmarks. The preventive measures section includes a new inspection checklist and an automated alert system. If we follow through on these recommendations, situations like this would not happen again. I have attached the full document for your review. Please add your feedback by Friday.

The team conducts a post-mortem of the APAC rollout failure. They perform a root cause analysis and identify vendor quality control issues. Fabiana drafts the lessons learned document with key takeaways. The team agrees on corrective actions and preventive measures. They set benchmarks for quarterly vendor audits going forward.
